Title: Ms.
Role_Requirements: Demonstrate strong operational oversight capabilities, including daily activity monitoring to ensure tasks are completed on schedule and aligned with project goals. They should be adept at coordinating participants, resolving on-site challenges, and fostering discipline and motivation. Effective resource management is essential, with responsibility for overseeing tools, materials, and equipment to prevent waste and promote efficiency. A thorough understanding of occupational health and safety standards is required, along with the ability to enforce compliance with SEF and government regulations. The role demands meticulous documentation skills to maintain accurate attendance, payroll, performance records, and administrative paperwork. Strong communication and stakeholder engagement are critical, as the candidate will serve as a liaison between project managers, implementing partners, and community participants, while also managing conflict resolution to sustain a productive work environment. Quality assurance is key, including tracking performance against targets and contributing to impact reporting that reflects the social value of the work. Leadership qualities are essential, particularly in mentoring youth and women to develop workplace skills and civic responsibility. Finally, the candidate must be a creative problem-solver, capable of adapting to challenges such as absenteeism, resource shortages, and shifting priorities with agility and innovation.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Ensuring administrative alignment across all SEF activities, systems, and reporting structures to maintain consistency and operational efficiency. It involves tracking participant time and attendance to support accurate stipend processing and performance monitoring. The candidate must be able to compile narrative reports that capture project impact, challenges, and community transformation. A key responsibility includes mentoring and supporting the administrative team across Gauteng, Mpumalanga, and KwaZulu-Natal to build capacity and ensure uniform practices. The role also entails organizing training and induction sessions for new participants to foster understanding, engagement, and readiness. Accuracy in documentation is essential, with oversight of contracts, onboarding forms, attendance registers, and compliance records to ensure completeness and regulatory adherence.

Title: Miss
Role_Requirements: Minimum requirement: National Senior Certificate (Matric). Skills and Competencies, Computer literacy: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and basic data entry systems.Organizational skills: Ability to manage files, schedules, and multiple administrative tasks efficiently. Attention to detail: Accuracy in capturing attendance, payroll data, and compliance documentation. Communication skills: Clear verbal and written communication for reporting and team coordination. Time management: Ability to meet deadlines and support multi-site operations.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Attendance tracking: Capture and maintain accurate daily attendance registers for participants across sites. Payroll support: Prepare and verify stipend documentation, ensuring timely and correct payments. Document management: Organize contracts, onboarding forms, ID copies, and compliance records. Support coordination: Assist Site Supervisors and the Project Coordinator with logistics, procurement records, and communication. Training logistics: Help organize induction and training sessions, including venue setup, materials, and participant lists.

Title: Mr.
Role_Requirements: Must have Grade 12, Computer Literacy, Attention to detail
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Planning and organising ECD sessions. Teaching assigned learners, and assisting them with reading, learning how to write, and Grade 1 preparation. Tracking learner attendance and undertaking administrative tasks related to the role.

Title: Ms.
Role_Requirements: Be a South African citizen. Not a recipient of SASSA SRD grant or NSFAS. Be between the ages of 18-34 . Reside locally (Wards 35 and 88). Be able to communicate fluently in both isiXhosa and English. Have passed Matric with these minimum codes: 50% for English FAL, 45% for Mathematical Literacy, or 40% for Mathematics. Having a tertiary qualification or teaching assistant experience is advantageous.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Support learners with their academic work (structured program for Foundation Phase and support with homework for higher Grades). Support selected learners with their academic work (structured program for Foundation Phase and support with homework for higher Grades). Deliver program as per the timetable; facilitate learning through a structured program; homework support – assist learners to complete their homework; assist learners with test and exam preparation; conducting educational games and programs aligned to their work; encourage peer support and mentoring in sessions; support and encourage learners to achieve academic success; motivation, mentoring of learners and modelling; optimal development of the learners to attain better education through seeking relevant intervention for issues that affect their learning capabilities; Support learners with activities that enrich them socially, emotionally, cognitively and spiritually, conducting club activities and coordinating other developmental activities, praying and doing spiritual growth activities or programs, planning of activities and implementation of plans, conducting clubs, holiday clubs, outings and camps; supporting all developmental, educational and recreational activities for primary school learners by assisting in sports, drama and dance, educational, creative arts & youth life skills programs; ensure that learners feel safe; ensure that learners attending the session get nutritional food; ensure the coordinator is given names and details of learners that need emotional, spiritual; ensure learner attendance registers are signed; ensure that follow up is done with teachers and parents where necessary; ensure your reports are done and submitted in time; ensure that you attend team sessions (weekly planning and reflection meetings, monthly meetings etc);

Title: Mr.
Role_Requirements: Be a South African citizen. Not a recipient of SASSA SRD grant or NSFAS. Be between the ages of 18-34. Reside locally (Wards 35 and 88). Be able to communicate fluently in both isiXhosa and English.Have passed Matric with these minimum codes: 50% for English FAL, 45% for Mathematical Literacy, or 40% for Mathematics. Having a tertiary qualification or teaching assistant experience is advantageous.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Support learners with their academic work (structured program for Foundation Phase and support with homework for higher Grades). Support selected learners with their academic work (structured program for Foundation Phase and support with homework for higher Grades). Deliver program as per the timetable; facilitate learning through a structured program; homework support – assist learners to complete their homework; assist learners with test and exam preparation; conducting educational games and programs aligned to their work; encourage peer support and mentoring in sessions; support and encourage learners to achieve academic success; motivation, mentoring of learners and modelling; optimal development of the learners to attain better education through seeking relevant intervention for issues that affect their learning capabilities; Support learners with activities that enrich them socially, emotionally, cognitively and spiritually, conducting club activities and coordinating other developmental activities, praying and doing spiritual growth activities or programs, planning of activities and implementation of plans, conducting clubs, holiday clubs, outings and camps; supporting all developmental, educational and recreational activities for primary school learners by assisting in sports, drama and dance, educational, creative arts & youth life skills programs; ensure that learners feel safe; ensure that learners attending the session get nutritional food; ensure the coordinator is given names and details of learners that need emotional, spiritual; ensure learner attendance registers are signed; ensure that follow up is done with teachers and parents where necessary; ensure your reports are done and submitted in time; ensure that you attend team sessions (weekly planning and reflection meetings, monthly meetings etc);

Also to collaborate with caregivers and educators to intergrate play based learning into existing programs. You are also required to pitch up at work on time and fullfil all that is required of you within working hours.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: You are required to complete up to 56 hours per month. During your period of service you will report to a Site Supervisor assigned by the host organisation, to obey and follow instructions given by them or any other person delegated by the host organisation. You may be required to perform other duties that may be reasonably expected of you within the host organisation from time to time. You are also responsible for correctly documenting or recording your sign in and signing out attendance and reporting any issues with the Kwantu app or timesheets.

Title: Ms.
Role_Requirements: An accredited education or coaching qualification would be preferable; Fluent in isiZulu and an ability to communicate in English; Open to learning and growth; Passion about the purpose and mission of iThemba Project; Computer skills and driver’s licence preferable; Able to collate reports and handle data for the purposes of M and E; Experience in managing teams or individuals would be preferable; The ability to work as part of a diverse team and to work cross culturally.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Attend and participate fully in all training as required by the organisations represented in this project. Participate in the recruitment, orientation and placement of facilitators as and when the need arises. Build trusting and respectful relationships with schools, parents, facilitators and learners involved in the project. Provide programmatic support to facilitators. Conduct a needs analysis for facilitators and implement a structured coaching and mentorship plan to address gaps identified. Liaise with schools to ensure that programme implementation runs smoothly – this includes, but is not limited to, problem identification and solving, as well as oversight of resources. Compile weekly implementation report for Project Coordinator. Ensure that programme administration is up to date at all times. Manage quality assurance measures through Monitoring and Evaluation. Visit each facilitator once a week during a session. Ensure program resources are available on site for implementation. Manage and audit the resource kit and submit requests for replenishing the resources through the appropriate channels and in a timeous manner. Maintain the TARL facilitators work space and storeroom at the iThemba Community Centre – a neat, clean, well-organised, inspiring work environment.

Title: Mr.
Role_Requirements: A desire to Pursue an education in Social work or education, Open to learning and Growth, Must have a Grade 12, Passionate about the purpose of iThemba Projects,Computer Skills and driver’s licence preferable, Fluent in isiZulu and an ability to communicate in English, Passionate about working with Children,The ability to work as part of diverse team and to work cross culturally.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Daily lesson planning and preparation done on site. Conduct daily literacy and numeracy sessions at 3-4 schools. Provide 60 sessions per learner focusing on Grade 4, 5, and 6 students. Collaborate with parents and educators as directed by the mentor or Project Coordinator. Maintain open and effective communication with each school, fostering strong relationships. Assess learners' progress and report using the required structures. Provide constructive feedback to learners and teachers to support continuous improvement. Complete monitoring and evaluation (M&E) tools to track learner attendance and progression. Manage and maintain resource kits necessary for facilitating the sessions.Ensure that all weekly paperwork is done and filing has been done correctly at the end of each week. This includes filing on the Local Area Network (LAN).Prepare for and participate in weekly team meetings. Assist with planning and hosting of visitors to iThemba (funders, stakeholders, international teams, volunteers).

Title: Mr.
Address: House Number 192, Mathibestad, Phase 2, Mathibestad, Mathibestad, South Africa
Role_Requirements: Youth between 19-35 years with the minimum of national senior certificate (NSC) Matric, up to Diploma and Degree qualification. Experience in educational environments as teaching assistants, facilitating, teaching, lesson planning, and tutoring numeracy. Familiarity and ease in working with children. Strong verbal and written communication skills English. Native languages will be a bonus. Ability to develop strong and cooperative working relationships with students, teachers, school leadership, local community-based stakeholders and members of the community. Demonstrate a proactive approach to self-development as well as strive to link theory with practice. In accordance with Youth Impact`s Child Safeguarding & Protection Policy, a criminal background check is compulsory upon hiring.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: We seek facilitators/peer educators who excel in a team-oriented and collaborative environment that values curiosity, a passion for improving youth`s lives and enjoy being in the classroom working with students. We are looking for candidates who can manage partner schools, organization of teachers and youth volunteer training, managing a successful high-impact program to measurably improve numeracy and literacy. The candidate will also enjoy problem solving and applying creative thinking to challenges and building relationship with teachers, program leaders, implementers, and government partners. To deliver our Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) program (teaching basic numeracy and literacy) to school learners in grade 3-6 in the Bojanala District, specifically in Moretele. Peer educators will be trained on the program assessment tools, curriculum; and operations and will implement the TaRL program over the course of the contract.

Title: Miss
Address: 415 Hans Kekana View, Temba, Hammanskraal, Pretoria, 0407, South Africa
Role_Requirements: Youth between 19-35 years with the minimum of national senior certificate (NSC) Matric, up to Diploma and Degree qualification. Experience in educational environments as teaching assistants, facilitating, teaching, lesson planning, and tutoring numeracy. Familiarity and ease in working with children. Strong verbal and written communication skills English. Native languages will be a bonus. Ability to develop strong and cooperative working relationships with students, teachers, school leadership, local community-based stakeholders and members of the community. Demonstrate a proactive approach to self-development as well as strive to link theory with practice. In accordance with Youth Impact`s Child Safeguarding & Protection Policy, a criminal background check is compulsory upon hiring.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: We seek facilitators/peer educators who excel in a team-oriented and collaborative environment that values curiosity, a passion for improving youth`s lives and enjoy being in the classroom working with students. We are looking for candidates who can manage partner schools, organization of teachers and youth volunteer training, managing a successful high-impact program to measurably improve numeracy and literacy. The candidate will also enjoy problem solving and applying creative thinking to challenges and building relationship with teachers, program leaders, implementers, and government partners. To deliver our Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) program (teaching basic numeracy and literacy) to school learners in grade 3-6 in the Bojanala District, specifically in Moretele. Peer educators will be trained on the program assessment tools, curriculum; and operations and will implement the TaRL program over the course of the contract.

Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Maintain and verify timesheets and attendance records for all staff and participants, input time and pay data into the payroll or attendance system, and track overtime and compensatory time earned. Calculate wages and statutory withholdings, update pay data promptly based on participant or staff changes, and reconcile any discrepancies between timesheets and payroll. Prepare and submit regular attendance and payroll reports to Site Supervisor and Finance, respond to timekeeping and payroll queries, ensure all records are accurate and stored securely, and meet payroll deadlines and internal data-protection requirements.
Site_LocationAddress: Diepkloof Library - 277 Jack Klipin Road Zone 1 Diepkloof

Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Deliver planned lessons and one-on-one tutoring to improve learners’ literacy, numeracy and study skills; prepare and adapt simple lesson notes and learning activities to learner needs; monitor and record learner attendance and progress; collect and report referrals for learners with learning or behavioural challenges; communicate regularly with parents, school staff, and the MEL team about learner progress; support and participate in extracurricular activities and school events; attend team meetings, trainings, and supervision sessions; maintain accurate session and learner records as required by MCOC.

Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Deliver planned lessons and one-on-one tutoring to improve learners’ literacy, numeracy and study skills; prepare and adapt simple lesson notes and learning activities to learner needs; monitor and record learner attendance and progress; collect and report referrals for learners with learning or behavioural challenges; communicate regularly with parents, school staff, and the MEL team about learner progress; support and participate in extracurricular activities and school events; attend team meetings, trainings, and supervision sessions; maintain accurate session and learner records as required by MCOC.

Title: Mr.
Role_Requirements: Hold no criminal record. Demonstrate craftsmanship with tools and machinery, forethought and care when using crop protection and fertilisers, physical stamina and fitness for manual outdoor work, and practical knowledge of nature, soil, plants, and the local environment. Follow health, safety and chemical-handling procedures, maintain reliable attendance, and work cooperatively with site staff.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Install and maintain seasonal plants and green spaces, mow, trim and fertilise lawns, and mulch, edge and weed garden beds. Prune and shape trees, shrubs and bushes, monitor plant health and respond to pest or disease problems using safe practices, and keep gardens and grounds free of debris and litter. Maintain and service gardening equipment and machinery, report equipment or supply needs, record maintenance activities and inputs, and coordinate with the Site Supervisor to schedule works and support community garden activities as required.

Title: Miss
Role_Requirements: The Communication Officer is responsible for developing, coordinating, and implementing RNDA’s internal and external communication activities. This includes managing media relations, creating content, enhancing RNDA’s visibility, and ensuring consistent messaging that reflects RNDA’s mission of building thriving neighbourhoods through education, environmental stewardship, and social innovation.
Role_DutiesResponsibilites:
Develop and implement RNDA’s annual Communication and Branding Strategy aligned with its vision and programmes (STEM, Market Gardens, Skills Training, etc.). Ensure brand consistency across all materials (logos, templates, signage, social media, and events). Support organisational storytelling by showcasing RNDA’s impact in communities. Write and edit engaging content for press releases, newsletters, social media, the RNDA website, brochures, and donor reports. Capture success stories, testimonials, and case studies from learners, tutors, and beneficiaries. Manage the creation of communication materials (flyers, posters, videos, newsletters, etc.). Build and maintain relationships with journalists, local media, and community radio stations. Draft and distribute media releases, fact sheets, and public statements. Coordinate media coverage for RNDA events, campaigns, and milestones. Manage RNDA’s digital platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.). Schedule and monitor posts to grow audience engagement. Track analytics to measure reach, engagement, and impact. Support internal communication between staff, tutors, and volunteers through updates, newsletters, and briefing notes. Develop templates and tools for consistent communication across programmes and teams.
Assist in planning and publicising key RNDA events such as: After-school programme launches and graduations. Fundraising drives and mall activation days. Community clean-up and Clear Rivers campaigns. Youth development workshops and parent evenings. Create event communication packs (posters, press releases, social media banners).Support communication with donors, partners, and government agencies. Draft thank-you messages, partnership announcements, and updates. Assist in preparing presentations and reports for external stakeholders. Maintain a communication calendar of planned activities and events. Track communication outputs (media mentions, social reach, newsletter metrics). Provide monthly or quarterly reports on communication achievements and challenges.

Title: Miss
Role_Requirements: The Finance Officer ensures sound financial management, accountability, and compliance across all RNDA programmes and projects.They are responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, managing budgets, processing payments, preparing reports, and supporting management in strategic decision-making — in line with the requirements of donors, partners, and statutory bodies (e.g., *SARS, DSD, and CIPC*).
Role_DutiesResponsibilites: Develop and monitor annual budgets in collaboration with the Executive Director and Programme Managers. Track income and expenditure for each project (STEM Programme, Market Gardens, Skills Training, etc.). Prepare budget variance analyses and recommend corrective action. Assist in proposal budgeting for donor applications and new initiatives. Maintain up-to-date and accurate financial records using approved accounting software or spreadsheets. Process all financial transactions, including receipts, payments, payroll, and petty cash. Reconcile bank statements, supplier accounts, and donor funding allocations. Ensure proper filing of all financial documents for audits and compliance checks. Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports for management, the board, and donors. Support the preparation of annual financial statements and coordinate external audits. Provide input for organisational performance reports, highlighting financial sustainability and risks.Ensure compliance with *SARS, DSD, CIPC*, and donor regulations. Implement and monitor internal control systems to prevent errors or misuse of funds. Maintain proper documentation for all financial activities in line with RNDA’s financial policy manual. Assist in updating and reviewing financial policies and procedures.Manage procurement in line with RNDA’s approved procedures — ensuring transparency and fairness. Prepare and process purchase orders, quotations, and supplier payments. Ensure all expenses are properly authorised and supported by valid documentation. Keep a vendor database and ensure timely payments to suppliers and service providers. Prepare and process monthly payroll (salaries, stipends, tutor payments, etc.). Ensure compliance with PAYE, UIF, and other statutory deductions. Maintain accurate staff financial records and liaise with HR on staff-related financial matters.Track utilisation restricted and unrestricted funds to ensure compliance with donor conditions. Prepare donor financial reports*in the required formats and timelines. Monitor cash flow and ensure sufficient funds are available for operational needs. Support fundraising and grant proposal teams with accurate financial data.

Project_Name: The Catch-Up Coalition: Social Employment Fund Project 4
Title: Miss
Role_Requirements:
Essential Qualifications & Experience
National Diploma or Degree in Accounting, Taxation, or related field
Minimum 2 years' experience in an accounting practice or similar environment
Working knowledge of South African tax legislation (Income Tax Act, VAT Act)
Proficiency with SARS eFiling platform
Experience with CIPC online services
Strong Microsoft Excel skills
Technical Knowledge
Understanding of IFRS for SMMEs and basic financial reporting requirements
Familiarity with the Companies Act, 2008
Knowledge of payroll compliance (PAYE, UIF, SDL, COID)
Experience with accounting software (Sage, Xero, QuickBooks, or similar)
Advantageous
Experience with AFS compilation software (Draftworx or Caseware)
NPO registration and compliance experience
SAIPA, SAIT, SAICA, or CIMA membership or articles
Experience serving SMME clients
Core Competencies
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Ability to explain complex tax and compliance matters in simple, accessible language
Empathy and professionalism when dealing with clients facing challenging circumstances
Self-motivated with strong time management and ability to meet deadlines
Problem-solving mindset with initiative to research and find solutions
Commitment to confidentiality and professional ethic
Role_DutiesResponsibilites:
Tax Compliance & SARS Administration
Prepare and submit individual tax returns (IT12) and company tax returns (IT14) via SARS eFiling
Process provisional tax calculations and submissions
Handle VAT registrations, returns, and vendor compliance
Apply for tax clearance certificates and manage SARS correspondence
Assist with PAYE/UIF/SDL registrations and reconciliations (EMP501). Support clients with SARS queries, payment arrangements, and dispute resolution
Company Secretarial & CIPC Compliance. Process company registrations (Pty Ltd, NPC). Prepare and submit CIPC annual returns
Handle director changes, share transfers, and company amendments
Maintain company records and compliance calendars
Bookkeeping & Financial Reporting. Perform monthly bookkeeping including transaction recording and bank reconciliations. Prepare management accounts and financial reports. Support Annual Financial Statement (AFS) compilation in accordance with IFRS for SMMEs.Assist with cash flow projections and financial analysis
Non-Profit Organisation (NPO) Services. Process NPO registrations with the Department of Social Development.Handle SARS Section 18A tax exemption applications
Prepare NPO annual narrative and financial reports
Maintain compliance with NPO regulatory requirements
Site_LocationAddress: 03 Fifth Avenue, Colridge View, Oudtshoorn, 6625/ 14 Plympton Road, Plumstead, Cape Town, 7800
